title: The 24/7 gate watcher reads probe-truth and PROPOSES — it structurally cannot apply routing, and that split is the whole safety design
summary: SpiderGate went reactive-manual -> self-healing (WS4: the server auto-evicts genuinely-dead slots behind a consecutive-probe-failure debounce). The 24/7 WATCHER is a separate, deliberately weaker actor: an OpenClaw agent ('Kevin') that every hour reads the probe-truth routing catalog, finds a delisted/no_tools/errored model sitting in a live alias, and FILES a Tier-2 routing proposal for a human to approve. The owner-lock (#6) is that this watcher can read + propose but NEVER set / roll back / approve a chain. That is enforced in two independent layers so it cannot be bypassed by prompt or bug: (1) SCOPE — the watcher's PAT carries only gate:routing:read + gate:routing:propose; the routing WRITE endpoints (PUT /aliases, POST /rollback, POST /proposals/{id}/approve) have NO scoped-PAT path at all (they require the master X-Admin-Key / super_admin), so the token is 403'd/absent by construction; (2) SURFACE — this skill (gate-health-watch) exposes only catalogStatus / listProposals / fileProposal; the set/rollback/approve methods live in the separate manage-routing skill (X-Admin-Key). fileProposal only inserts a status='pending', tier=2 row (current_chain resolved server-side so the caller cannot spoof state) and is idempotent per alias via the migration-333 partial unique index. Two probe-truth traps the watcher must respect, inherited from self_heal: last_probe_status collapses every transient (429/5xx/auth/timeout) into 'error' and free ':free' slugs flap ok<->error every cycle, so ONLY delisted/no_tools are deterministic-dead — a lone 'error' must be debounced (persistent across ticks or corroborated by key-failures/error-rate) or it wastes a human review; and a chain is a least-busy POOL not a priority list, so the fix is to REMOVE/REPLACE the dead slot, never to pad the pool with a redundant tail. The lesson for anyone extending gate autonomy: keep the propose/apply split as a scope AND surface split, and make the watcher act off the probe catalog (the truth) not the chain listing (which lies about delisted slots).
references: app/api/v1/admin_gate/routing.py — POST /routing/proposals (create_proposal): scoped gate:routing:propose, inserts tier=2 status=pending via the same ON CONFLICT (alias) WHERE status='pending' DO NOTHING insert self_heal uses; the security note (lines ~60-75) documents that routing WRITES have no PAT path
app/core/admin_scopes.py — GATE_ROUTING_READ + GATE_ROUTING_PROPOSE added to ADMIN_SCOPES (WS3b); GATE_ROUTING_WRITE stays master-key for set/rollback/approve

app/services/gate/self_heal.py — the debounce (consecutive_probe_failures >= GATE_SELFHEAL_DEAD_PROBE_STREAK, mig 431) + classify_probe collapsing transients into 'error'; the watcher mirrors that debounce before proposing

SpiderGate LEARNINGS — 'probe status is the truth not GET /models', 'OpenRouter :free slugs flap ok<->error', 'a chain is a least-busy pool not a priority list'; gate-reliability-observability plan WS3b (board ee38dd3d)
manage-routing skill (@spideriq/admin-skills) — the human super-admin surface (X-Admin-Key) that APPROVES the proposals this watcher files; deliberately separate from this skill
